attributes.description: Alerts when `postgresql.deadlocks > 0` per database. Concurrency issues in the application.
attributes.name: [PostgreSQL OTel] Deadlocks detected

investigation_guide.blob:
## PostgreSQL deadlocks detected

### What fired
`postgresql.deadlocks` increased during the window.

### Why it matters
Deadlocks abort a transaction to break a cycle. Sustained deadlocks point to inconsistent access order.

### Triage
1. Inspect server log for deadlock details.
2. Look at application access patterns.

### Remediation
- Fix access ordering.

### Tuning
- Fires on any non-zero count.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-postgresqlreceiver.otel-*
// Deadlocks are database-level monotonic counter; any increase indicates concurrency issues
| WHERE postgresql.deadlocks IS NOT NULL
| EVAL deadlocks_val = TO_DOUBLE(postgresql.deadlocks)
| WHERE deadlocks_val > 0
// Group by instance and database for targeted alerting
| STATS deadlock_count = SUM(deadlocks_val) BY service.instance.id, postgresql.database.name
| WHERE deadlock_count > 0
